title: A Big Tennis Upset: Korda Beats Alcaraz
summary: In a surprising tennis match at the Miami Open, Sebastian Korda beat Carlos Alcaraz, who is the world's number one player. Alcaraz started poorly in the first set, but he fought back to win the second set. In the third set, Korda stayed calm and broke Alcaraz's serve to take the lead. He then won the match on his second match point. After the game, Alcaraz said he is still improving and feels good about his progress, even though he lost. He mentioned that lower-ranked players like Korda can play without pressure against him. Korda, ranked 36th, will now play another match in the tournament. This was a big win for Korda because Alcaraz is a top player who won the Australian Open earlier this year.

background_read: Tennis is a popular sport played worldwide, where players compete in tournaments like the Miami Open. The world number one is the top-ranked player based on points from matches. Upsets happen when a lower-ranked player wins, making games exciting. Players often talk about pressure, which means feeling nervous during important moments. After a match, players might rest before the next tournament, like the clay-court season.
Article_Structure: WHO: Sebastian Korda and Carlos Alcaraz, two tennis players
WHAT: Korda beat Alcaraz in a surprising match at the Miami Open
WHERE: At the Miami Open tennis tournament
WHY: Korda played well and stayed calm, while Alcaraz had a sloppy start and felt pressure from being number one

perspectives.description: Alcaraz thinks he is improving despite the loss and notes that lower-ranked players like Korda play without pressure.
